page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter in October: Shoulder Season Weather & Deals

faq.q: Is October a good time for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes. October is shoulder season: warm air (22-26°C), pleasant sea temps (20-24°C), and far fewer tourists. You get better availability on boats like the Azimut 39 or Mangusta 80, plus lower rates than July–August.

faq.q: What is the weather like for a Marbella yacht charter in October?
faq.a: Expect mostly sunny days with highs around 24°C. Sea breezes keep it comfortable, and rain is rare (only 3-4 wet days on average). The water is still swimmable, especially in the first half of the month.

faq.q: Are there any special deals for October charters?
faq.a: Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for a full 8-hour day – all inclusive. Tier B Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 with a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boats are available for a October charter from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Our full fleet departs Puerto Banús: from the speedy Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax). Popular October choices include the Mangusta 80 (12 pax, free jet ski) and the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax). All include skipper, fuel, and drinks.

faq.q: Can I swim in the sea off Marbella in October?
faq.a: Yes. Sea temperatures average 20-24°C in October – refreshing but not cold. Many guests swim at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. The water is generally calm, making it safe for snorkelling or paddleboarding.

faq.q: What are the best destinations for a October yacht charter?
faq.a: Popular routes include cruising to Cabopino’s sand dunes, anchoring off Estepona’s old town, or visiting Sotogrande’s marina. Sunset trips towards La Concha mountain are also stunning in the lower autumn sun.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht charter in Marbella for October?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on any boat. Tier A and Tier B boats can be booked directly through our site. We recommend booking 1-2 weeks ahead to secure your preferred date.

faq.q: What is included in the October charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras – just turn up at Puerto Banús.

data.body_html:
October is Marbella’s best-kept secret for yacht charters. The summer crowds have melted away, the Mediterranean stays warm at 20–24°C, and you can book a boat from Puerto Banús at shoulder-season rates. Whether you want a 2-hour sunset spin or a full day exploring the Costa del Sol, autumn delivers calm seas, golden light, and genuine value.

Why Choose a Marbella Yacht Charter in October?

October sits in the sweet spot between the peak summer heat and the cooler winter months. Average air temperatures hover around 22–26°C – perfect for lounging on the foredeck without baking. The sea is still warm enough for swimming, especially in the first half of the month. And with fewer boats in the water, you’ll often have coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés almost to yourself.

Another advantage: availability. Boats that book out weeks in advance during July and August – like the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge – are far easier to reserve in October. You can often secure a last-minute charter or negotiate a longer trip at a better price.

October Weather & Sea Conditions for Yacht Charters

Don’t let the idea of autumn put you off. October in Marbella is typically sunny, with only 3–4 rainy days across the month. The wind is generally light (Beaufort 1–3), which means smooth cruising for even first-time charter guests. Sea temperatures remain above 20°C until late October, so swimming, snorkelling, and paddleboarding are all on the cards.

Sunset comes earlier – around 19:30 – which makes for spectacular evening charters. The low sun paints La Concha mountain in shades of orange and pink, and the harbour lights of Puerto Banús begin to twinkle as you return to port.

Our Fleet: What’s Available for October

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús. Here’s a quick overview of what you can charter:

BoatLengthMax GuestsNotes
Astondoa 4012.5m9Tier A – €749/2h to €2,299/8h
Azimut 3912.5m11Tier A – same pricing
Mangusta 80 (any colour)24m12Tier B – €4,719 min 4h, free jet ski
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12Pricing on request via WhatsApp
Ferretti 9429m12Pricing on request via WhatsApp

For a full list, visit our boat rental Marbella page. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, drinks, and snacks – no hidden costs.

Best October Itineraries from Puerto Banús

With shorter days, you’ll want to make the most of your time on the water. Here are three recommended routes:

  • Sunset Cruise to Cabopino: Depart at 16:00, cruise east along the coast to the sand dunes of Cabopino. Anchor for a swim, enjoy drinks as the sun sets, and return by 19:30. Perfect on a Tier A boat.
  • Estepona & Cala del Faro: Head west from Puerto Banús towards Estepona. Stop at Cala del Faro for a quiet swim, then lunch in Estepona’s marina. This trip works well on a Mangusta 80 with the free jet ski.
  • Sotogrande Day Trip: For a longer day (8 hours), cruise to Sotogrande. The marina there is exclusive, and the route passes the Golden Mile and Río Verde. Ideal on the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94.

October Deals & Pricing

Shoulder season means real savings. Our Tier A boats – the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 – start at €749 for a 2-hour charter and cap at €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. That includes the skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and all taxes. For a larger group, the Mangusta 80 (Tier B) is available from €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours, and you get a jet ski thrown in for free.

Other boats in our fleet – like the Canados 86, Fairline Targa, or the 26m Maiora – are priced on request. Just drop us a message on WhatsApp and we’ll send you a custom quote within hours.

What’s Included in Every October Charter

We keep things simple: no hidden fees, no upsells. Every booking includes:

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (crisps, nuts, olives)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% VAT)

You just need to bring sunscreen, a towel, and your camera.

How to Book Your October Yacht Charter

Ready to get on the water? For Tier A and Tier B boats, you can book directly through our Marbella boat rental page. For any other boat – or if you have specific questions – contact us on WhatsApp. We recommend booking at least one week ahead in October to secure your preferred vessel and time slot.

October is a wonderful month to experience the Costa del Sol from the sea. Warm weather, calm waters, and unbeatable value – it’s the shoulder season that feels like a secret.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is October a good time for a yacht charter in Marbella?

Yes. October is shoulder season: warm air (22-26°C), pleasant sea temps (20-24°C), and far fewer tourists. You get better availability on boats like the Azimut 39 or Mangusta 80, plus lower rates than July–August.

What is the weather like for a Marbella yacht charter in October?

Expect mostly sunny days with highs around 24°C. Sea breezes keep it comfortable, and rain is rare (only 3-4 wet days on average). The water is still swimmable, especially in the first half of the month.

Are there any special deals for October charters?

Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for a full 8-hour day – all inclusive. Tier B Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 with a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Which boats are available for a October charter from Puerto Banús?

Our full fleet departs Puerto Banús: from the speedy Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax). Popular October choices include the Mangusta 80 (12 pax, free jet ski) and the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax). All include skipper, fuel, and drinks.

Can I swim in the sea off Marbella in October?

Yes. Sea temperatures average 20-24°C in October – refreshing but not cold. Many guests swim at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. The water is generally calm, making it safe for snorkelling or paddleboarding.

What are the best destinations for a October yacht charter?

Popular routes include cruising to Cabopino’s sand dunes, anchoring off Estepona’s old town, or visiting Sotogrande’s marina. Sunset trips towards La Concha mountain are also stunning in the lower autumn sun.

How do I book a yacht charter in Marbella for October?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on any boat. Tier A and Tier B boats can be booked directly through our site. We recommend booking 1-2 weeks ahead to secure your preferred date.

What is included in the October charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras – just turn up at Puerto Banús.
